response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong distributed systems and cloud architecture experience with proven technical leadership capabilities, they fall significantly short on the critical minimum qualification of 8 years of C++ programming experience. Their professional C++ work is limited to approximately 6 months at National Instruments (Dec 2014-June 2015), whereas the role explicitly requires 8 years. Cloud compute platform roles typically demand deep systems programming expertise, and this gap represents a substantial barrier that would require significant ramp-up time.
strengths: Proven experience building and maintaining large-scale distributed systems handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby
Demonstrated technical leadership as Staff engineer with track record of establishing engineering best practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) and mentoring others
Solid cloud architecture and DevOps background including AWS CDK, Kubernetes, Docker, and infrastructure automation
concerns: Critical gap in C++ expertise—only ~6 months of professional experience versus the 8-year minimum requirement, which is fundamental for systems programming in cloud compute platforms
No clear evidence of formal leadership experience specifically within cloud/DevOps engineering teams, despite having held Staff roles in other domains
Career shows broad full-stack generalist specialization rather than focused depth in systems programming and cloud compute platform architecture

key_factors.factor: Distributed systems and large-scale infrastructure experience
